task_yaml.task:
Please prepare a short planning note for Rust Global: Tokyo using real public
websites, not a local simulated event page.

Use these real sources:

- Event page: https://rustfoundation.org/event/rust-global-tokyo/
- Event background: https://rustfoundation.org/media/rust-global-tokyo-connecting-the-rust-ecosystem-across-industries-regions/
- Venue/travel page: https://events.linuxfoundation.org/open-source-summit-japan/attend/venue-travel/
- Route estimate from Shibuya Station to Toranomon Station:
  https://www.rome2rio.com/s/Shibuya-Station/Toranomon-Station

Also create a calendar event yourself in the real desktop calendar application
installed by the service, preferably GNOME Calendar. Do not use a mock
calendar, a local event simulator, or any script that creates/records the
event for you.

The calendar event should start exactly 40 minutes before the event start time.
Save auditable calendar evidence under `/tmp_workspace/results/`:

- `/tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_calendar_event.json`
- `/tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_calendar_export.ics`
- `/tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_calendar_screenshot.png`

The calendar screenshot should show the saved event in the real calendar
application or a saved event-details view. An import preview or unsaved
dialog is not enough by itself.

Then use a real graphical text editor to write:

    /tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_route_note.md

Preserve auditable evidence that a real graphical editor was used by saving a
screenshot of the note open in that editor:

- `/tmp_workspace/results/rust_meetup_note_editor_screenshot.png`

Also save browser evidence showing the real public pages you used:

- `/tmp_workspace/results/browser_event_page.png`
- `/tmp_workspace/results/browser_route_page.png`

The note must have exactly these three sections:

1. Event info
2. Route estimate
3. Arrival plan

Include the event time, venue, registration/cost requirement, the commute
estimate from Shibuya Station, and a practical arrival/departure plan. Do not
just copy the event-page introduction; synthesize the details into a useful
note. Use the route values from the real route and venue pages, not a generic
map estimate or guess.

Finally save `/tmp_workspace/results/gui_evidence.md` listing the real browser,
calendar, and text-editor windows you used and the screenshot paths.
